creator: yds@dppl.com
text:
Hi David,
 
 Heh.. as luck would have it I picked the same night to do my Postfix
 hacking that you were making changes on.  Oh well.  As long as you don't
 make any more changes I'll have a new patch for you by tomorrow that
 will apply cleanly to the port as it is now.
 
 Thanks for pointing out the INTERACTIVE=YES issue.  That flag is only
 necessary when the build or configure process itself is interactive
 versus us throwing a dialog based wrapper to configure options.  I know
 that's not a clear distinction so let me give you an example.  Try
 building mail/procmail or net/net-snmp.  Both of them will require you
 to type something during the configure or build process.  They do
 require INTERACTIVE=YES and a workaround for when BATCH is set.  We only
 need to deal with the latter.  And that's something I didn't test.  The
 new scripts/configure.postfix does account for BATCH being set but it is
 not being passed via SCRIPT_ENV.  I'll deal with it.
 
 When doing BATCH builds I'm defaulting to building WITH_PCRE only. 
 Simply because there's no downside or gotcha I can think of when doing
 so.  Similar to how www/mod_php defaults to zlib and MySQL.  Having PCRE
 in Postfix doesn't hurt, does not make any runtime dependency on the
 devel/pcre port, and I can't think of why not to do it.  mail/exim is
 also built with pcre.  The other options do create runtime dependencies
 on other ports therefore I don't think they should be included in a
 BATCH build.
 
 What I'm not clear on is how to pass the flag to build cyrus-sasl with
 DB3 when building Postfix with DB3.  cyrus-sasl does have hooks for that
 but I'm not sure how to use them.  If anybody does know -- pass it on.
 
 -- 
 Yarema
 
 "David W. Chapman Jr." wrote:
 > 
 > I made some changes that broke the Makefile portion of your patch, could you
 > update your patch?  Also don't we have to set something like INTERACTIVE=YES
 > so that this doesn't get created as a package on the cd set.  I'm very vague
 > on this, but remember hearing it.
